_meta.purpose: Structured local context inputs for the per-city content rewrite phase. Consumed by the rewrite prompt to produce bespoke intros per (city, service) pair on the 52 surviving service x location pages and 37 city hubs.
_meta.rules: Every factual field here is sourced and verifiable. Named entities (estates, conservation areas, parishes, developers) must be accurate. Where a fact was not confidently verified, the field value is the string 'verify' and the rewrite prompt is instructed to skip that fact. Do not fabricate planning authority names, AONB coverage, conservation area designations, named developers, or notable streets. When in doubt, leave a field blank or 'verify' rather than guess.

fields.district: Local planning authority (the borough/district council that handles planning applications).
fields.aonb: Relationship to Surrey Hills AONB (National Landscape). 'in' = sits within the AONB. 'edge' = on the boundary or partially within. 'adjacent' = close to but outside. 'none' = no meaningful proximity.
fields.green_belt: Relationship to Metropolitan Green Belt. 'in' = village or much of the settlement sits within. 'surrounded' = settlement edge is defined by Green Belt. 'none' = not applicable.
fields.conservation_area: Does the settlement have one or more designated conservation areas? true/false/partial. Named areas listed in conservation_area_named.
fields.conservation_area_named: Named conservation areas within the settlement. Empty array if none.
fields.dominant_material: The gate material with the strongest local demand and appropriateness. Options: 'wrought-iron', 'mild-steel-painted', 'aluminium', 'hardwood-iroko', 'hardwood-oak', 'accoya', 'mixed'. Multiple allowed.
fields.local_context: Short list of verifiable local context notes. Named estates, planning quirks, property archetypes, transport features. Each entry should be a standalone factual phrase.
fields.sloped_driveway_prevalence: 'common', 'variable', 'rare'. Based on topography: North Downs and Surrey Hills cities = common; flat commuter belt = rare.
fields.price_bracket: 'ultra-prime', 'premium', 'upper-mid', 'mid', 'commuter'. Reflects typical gate installation spend in the market, not house prices.

abinger-hammer.name: Abinger Hammer
local_context: Sits in the Vale of Holmesdale between Dorking and Guildford within the Surrey Hills AONB
The River Tillingbourne flows through the village
Adjacent to Leith Hill and the Greensand Ridge
Part of Abinger civil parish, Mole Valley district
Rural character with listed buildings, tight conservation controls on boundary treatments

ashtead.name: Ashtead
local_context: Affluent commuter village in Mole Valley between Leatherhead and Epsom
Home to Ashtead Common National Nature Reserve, a 200-acre ancient wood pasture
Significant Edwardian and inter-war residential stock with established gardens
Fast rail links to London Waterloo (about 45 minutes)

banstead.name: Banstead
local_context: Sits on the North Downs within the Reigate and Banstead borough
Banstead Downs and Banstead Heath are large open spaces under Green Belt protection
Established 1930s to 1950s residential development with generous plot sizes
High proportion of detached properties with gate-ready carriage drives

brockham.name: Brockham
local_context: Picturesque village with one of Surrey's largest traditional village greens
Sits at the foot of Box Hill within the Surrey Hills AONB
The River Mole flows through the village
Strong conservation controls around the green and period cottages

camberley.name: Camberley
local_context: Home to the Royal Military Academy Sandhurst (just over the Berkshire border)
Significant modern residential development and active housing market
M3 junction and direct rail to London Waterloo
Mixed housing stock from Victorian villas to modern new-build estates

caterham.name: Caterham
local_context: Sits on the southern slopes of the North Downs within the Surrey Hills AONB
Historically a Guards depot and Victorian spa town
Steep hillside streets create sloped driveway geometry
Conservation area coverage in older parts of the town

chiddingfold.name: Chiddingfold
local_context: The majority of the civil parish lies within the Surrey Hills AONB
Village core occupies a Low Weald valley on heavy Wealden clay
Historic village centred on The Green, with Grade I 13th-century St Mary's Church and the Grade II* Crown Inn
One of the largest parishes in Surrey at approximately 2,800 hectares
Chiddingfold Forest is a Site of Special Scientific Interest
Conservation area originally designated 1973 under the Civic Amenities Act

cobham.name: Cobham
local_context: One of the most exclusive residential addresses in Surrey, in Elmbridge district
Named private gated estates include Fairmile Estate, Eaton Park Estate, Oxshott Way Estate, and Burwood Park
Fairmile Avenue, Mizen Way, Icklingham Road, and Eaton Park Road are among the most exclusive addresses
Cobham and Stoke d'Abernon station runs direct to London Waterloo in about 38 minutes
Chelsea FC's training ground is at Stoke d'Abernon
ACS International School and Danes Hill School attract international buyers
Painshill Park is a Grade I listed 18th-century landscape garden

cranleigh.name: Cranleigh
local_context: Often described as one of the largest villages in England
Sits in the Low Weald south of the Surrey Hills AONB
Rural character with significant agricultural hinterland
Strong craft and independent retail high street

dorking.name: Dorking
local_context: Market town surrounded by the Surrey Hills AONB
Box Hill, Ranmore Common, and Leith Hill all within the Dorking hinterland
Leith Hill at 294m is the highest point in south-east England
The River Mole runs through the town via the famous North Downs gap
Significant listed building stock, Grade I Dorking Caves

dunsfold.name: Dunsfold
local_context: Rural Waverley village with one of Surrey's classic village greens
Grade I 13th-century Church of St Mary and All Saints
Dunsfold Aerodrome (former Top Gear test track) is nearby
Low Weald clay geology, traditional timber-framed cottages

elstead.name: Elstead
local_context: Waverley village on the River Wey between Farnham and Godalming
Grade I St James's Church dating from the 13th century
Thursley Common National Nature Reserve is adjacent
Traditional village green and surrounding listed cottages

epsom.name: Epsom
local_context: Administrative centre of Epsom and Ewell borough
Home to Epsom Downs Racecourse and the Derby Festival
Extensive Edwardian and inter-war detached housing stock
Epsom Common is a Site of Special Scientific Interest

esher.name: Esher
local_context: One of the three stockbroker-belt towns of Elmbridge (with Weybridge and Cobham)
Sandown Park Racecourse is within the town
Claremont Landscape Garden is a Grade I listed 18th-century landscape
Fast rail to London Waterloo in under 30 minutes
Premium detached residential stock on large plots

ewell.name: Ewell
local_context: Historic village that has retained its centre around the spring-fed Hogsmill River source
Bourne Hall is a Grade II listed building housing the local museum
Nonsuch Park is a Grade II* registered historic park
Mixed housing stock from period cottages to 1930s detached

farnham.name: Farnham
local_context: Historic Georgian market town on the Surrey-Hampshire border, within the Surrey Hills AONB
388 listed buildings in the town centre
44 Grade II* listed buildings in Farnham, the highest concentration in Waverley district
Castle Street has 17 Grade II* listed buildings, described by architectural historian Alec Clifton-Taylor as one of England's most visually delightful streets
Farnham Castle, Grade I listed, built 1138 AD by Henry de Blois
Strong craft heritage, home to the University for the Creative Arts and the Crafts Study Centre

fetcham.name: Fetcham
local_context: Village adjoining Leatherhead in Mole Valley district
On the edge of the Surrey Hills AONB near Box Hill
Fetcham Downs is a Site of Special Scientific Interest
Affluent commuter belt with substantial detached housing stock

godalming.name: Godalming
local_context: Historic market town in Waverley, within the Surrey Hills AONB
The first town in the world to have electric street lighting (1881)
Significant listed building stock in the centre
Home to Charterhouse School

gomshall.name: Gomshall
local_context: Village in the Shere civil parish, Guildford borough
Sits within the Surrey Hills AONB on the River Tillingbourne
On the North Downs Way between Shere and Dorking
Rural character, predominantly period and rural residential stock

guildford.name: Guildford
local_context: County town of Surrey, sits within the Surrey Hills AONB
The High Street runs up a steep hill from the River Wey
Guildford Castle (Grade I) and the historic cobbled High Street
Mix of period centre and substantial modern residential suburbs
Named by Experian in 2013 as the luxury shopping capital of the UK

haslemere.name: Haslemere
local_context: Surrey Hills AONB town on the Surrey-West Sussex-Hampshire border
Adjacent to the Devil's Punch Bowl, a natural amphitheatre of heathland
Hindhead and Haslemere are the most elevated towns in Surrey
Exposed rural positions favour Accoya and high-grade hardwood
Strong craft and arts heritage

horley.name: Horley
local_context: Commuter town north of Gatwick Airport
Major new-build developments include The Acres (710 homes by Barratt, David Wilson Homes, and Bovis Homes) and Westvale Park (1,510 homes by Crest Nicholson, Persimmon, Taylor Wimpey, and A2Dominion)
Direct rail to London Victoria
Metropolitan Green Belt surrounds the town

kingswood.name: Kingswood
local_context: Affluent Reigate and Banstead village between Tadworth and Lower Kingswood
Home to Kingswood Golf Club and significant golf course estate properties
Extensive detached housing stock with large plots and carriage drives
Adjacent to Walton Heath, one of England's premier golf courses

leatherhead.name: Leatherhead
local_context: Mole Valley town on the River Mole, at the famous gap in the North Downs
Administrative centre of Mole Valley district
Historic town centre with listed buildings
Adjacent to Box Hill and the Surrey Hills AONB to the south

leigh.name: Leigh
local_context: Small rural village in Reigate and Banstead borough, south of Reigate
Traditional village green and surrounding period cottages
Low Weald clay geology
Predominantly agricultural setting with detached rural properties

lightwater.name: Lightwater
local_context: Surrey Heath village between Bagshot and Camberley
Lightwater Country Park is a 58-hectare open space with heathland habitat
Predominantly post-war and modern residential development
Convenient access to M3 junction 3

lingfield.name: Lingfield
local_context: Tandridge village near the Surrey-Kent-Sussex border
Home to Lingfield Park Racecourse
Historic village centre with medieval church and Grade II* listed buildings
Low Weald geology, traditional rural-residential character

oxted.name: Oxted
local_context: Market town on the southern slopes of the North Downs within the Surrey Hills AONB
Administrative centre of Tandridge district
Old Oxted conservation area retains medieval village character
Direct rail to London Victoria and London Bridge
Metropolitan Green Belt surrounds the town

peaslake.name: Peaslake
local_context: Village in the Shere civil parish, Guildford borough
Sits at the heart of the Surrey Hills AONB adjacent to the 3,000-acre Hurtwood Forest
A mecca for walkers and mountain bikers via the Hurtwood tracks
Dense conservation area protection; very strict controls on boundary and gate design
Home to the Hurtwood Inn and the locally famous Peaslake Village Stores

redhill.name: Redhill
local_context: Identified in the Reigate and Banstead local plan as a focus for growth and regeneration
Direct rail to London Victoria and Gatwick
Mixed housing stock from Victorian terraces to major new-build developments
Metropolitan Green Belt surrounds the town

reigate.name: Reigate
local_context: Historic market town at the foot of the North Downs within the Surrey Hills AONB
Reigate Castle (now ruins within Castle Grounds) and Priory Park at the town's core
Listed building stock concentrated around the High Street and Bell Street
Metropolitan Green Belt surrounds the town
Direct rail to London Victoria and London Bridge

shere.name: Shere
local_context: Village in Guildford borough at the heart of the Surrey Hills AONB
Widely described as one of England's most beautiful villages
Used as a filming location for 'The Holiday' (2006)
Grade I listed St James's Church and Grade II* listed cottages along Middle Street and Lower Street
Conservation area with very strict boundary and gate design controls
Rural character with the Tillingbourne running through the village

tadworth.name: Tadworth
local_context: Affluent Reigate and Banstead village between Banstead and Kingswood
Adjacent to Walton Heath Golf Club, which has hosted the European Open
Established detached residential stock with generous plots
Surrounded by Green Belt including Walton Downs and the North Downs

walton-on-thames.name: Walton-on-Thames
local_context: Thames-side town in Elmbridge district adjacent to Weybridge
Riverside detached and period housing stock
Ashley Park, Burwood Park (partly), and Oatlands are affluent residential enclaves
Direct rail to London Waterloo in about 30 minutes

weybridge.name: Weybridge
local_context: The most exclusive of the Elmbridge stockbroker-belt towns, often referred to as British Beverly Hills
St George's Hill is a 964-acre private gated estate with around 450 homes, established 1911 by builder W G Tarrant, access controlled by 24-hour manned security
St George's Hill has ANPR and controlled card access at secondary gates; minimum plot size is one acre with 20 percent build limit
Burwood Park is a second significant private estate in Weybridge
Direct rail to London Waterloo in about 30 minutes
Home to Brooklands Museum on the site of the original Brooklands motor racing circuit

witley.name: Witley
local_context: Waverley village within the Surrey Hills AONB, between Godalming and Haslemere
Grade I All Saints Church dating from the 11th century
Witley Park, a Victorian country house estate, is nearby
Rural character with substantial period property stock
Home to King Edward's School Witley

woking.name: Woking
local_context: Largest town in west Surrey, major commuter hub
Direct rail to London Waterloo in about 25 minutes
The Shah Jahan Mosque (1889) is the UK's first purpose-built mosque
Significant modern residential development around the town centre
Metropolitan Green Belt surrounds the town
